references.name: Flexport
references.takeaway: Build a dual-field origin/destination input block as the hero element, styled like a shipping waybill with labeled fields, tracking numbers, and a route-map visualization below it showing the flow path.

implementation.description: The dashboard hero is a dual-field origin/destination input block styled as a shipping waybill. Labeled ORIGIN and DESTINATION fields with monospaced placeholder text (SHANGHAI, CN / ROTTERDAM, NL) sit side by side with an arrow separator. A TRACK SHIPMENT button follows. Below, the step pipeline shows the shipment stage progression, and the route map view provides the flow path visualization.

references.name: Maersk
references.takeaway: Position a prominent tracking/search bar as the top-of-page hero with bold label typography, and organize content below into industry-sector cards with flat-icon illustrations, creating a freight-terminal dispatch board layout.

implementation.element: tracking hero + gauge cluster + ideas cards
implementation.description: The tracking hero search bar sits as the primary above-fold interaction element with bold Saira Condensed label typography. The gauge cluster below uses circular meters with industrial readouts (throughput, on-time rate, capacity, fleet utilization) creating a dispatch board feel. The ideas view uses flat SVG icon illustrations in a card grid layout inspired by Maersk's sector carousel approach.

references.name: Freightos
references.takeaway: Use dense comparison tables with zebra-striped rows and a horizontal step-indicator pipeline at the top of each content section, making the layout feel like a logistics operations dashboard tracking cargo through stages.

implementation.description: The horizontal step-indicator pipeline (BOOKED > PICKED UP > IN TRANSIT > CUSTOMS > DELIVERED) appears at the top of the dashboard. Both the dispatch log table and the manifests table use dense columns with zebra-striped rows (alternating background on even rows). The manifest table includes tracking numbers, weight, destination, status, and progress bar columns — directly reflecting Freightos's rate comparison table density.
